text:
Once upon an autumn afternoon, little Olive wanted nothing more than to wander outdoors. Her big brother didn Ox chuckled good-naturedly.

"Dang Oliva, why do you always want to wander off?"

"Just watch me," Olive said proudly.

She picked up a pair of old farmer gloves. Why pick ones without fingers?

She wove silk pelicans into them.

She donned them.

Out bird-watched!

She couldn't stop herself. She'd weave feathers into everything.

Ox laughed till his sides ache. Olive had woven herself a pair of ornate feathered gloves.

Now she'd only walk through trees.

They prowled forests deep.

Just maybe one less owl.

She didn't count stars.

You get your treats.

That night, a fox took them.

Ox chased. Fox wouldn't.

They hunted fox.

One wouldn't mind a feathered glove.

So old.

So warm.

So comfortable.

They couldn't give that to him.

The end.<|endoftext|>
